response: convert the initial tax on the art collection is calculated by applying a 5% discount to the 20% rate, resulting in an effective rate of 19% on the $2 million value, which equals $380,000. convert the tax for the real estate portfolio at its original $5 million valuation is determined by summing 10% of the first $1 million, 15% of the next $2 million, and 20% of the remaining $2 million, totaling $1.1 million. convert the securities portfolio tax is found by subtracting the $100,000 charitable deduction from the $3 million value and applying the 15% rate to the resulting $2.9 million, yielding $435,000. convert the total initial tax liability combines these three figures to reach a sum of $1.915 million. convert the real estate portfolio must be revalued by increasing the original $5 million by 10% to reflect the correct market value of $5.5 million. convert the new tax on the real estate is computed by applying 10% to the first $1 million, 15% to the next $2 million, and 20% to the remaining $2.5 million, which results in a new liability of $1.2 million. convert the updated total tax liability for the entire estate is the sum of the unchanged art and securities taxes plus the new real estate tax, equaling $2.015 million.
